meta.note: Canonical roadmap for FeedHacker — committed work only (ideas not yet committed live in the Discussions doc). Status: pending = committed, in progress or upcoming · closed = shipped and live. IDs are permanent; only status/version change as work moves. Categories: filter, ai, ux, engine, infra, bug.

items.name: Autonomous AI-slop self-calibration
items.description: A living model that reviews the posts you see and re-tunes its own weights and threshold on-device — down-weighting tells that fire on most of the feed and setting the cutoff from the score distribution — so only the sloppiest slice hides, no clicking required. Your corrections nudge it secondarily.

items.name: Aggression slider that sticks
items.description: The AI-slop slider now sets the target share of the feed to hide (which the self-tuning honors) instead of a raw threshold it used to overwrite each cycle, so moving it persists.

items.name: AI-slop decision log
items.description: A local, exportable record of every post flagged and why — probability, tells that fired with weights, matched banlist phrases, author, a short preview, and your verdict — plus recalibrate-now and clear-log controls.

items.name: Group runs of hidden posts
items.description: 3+ consecutive hidden posts fold into one summary row ("N posts hidden · AI Slop ×8, Promoted ×3 · Show all"); isolated hidden posts still show a normal stub. Popup toggle. Replaces the removed digest mode.

items.name: Click-safe re-apply + faster load-more
items.description: Stub clicks are handled by one delegated listener so they survive LinkedIn's React re-renders; the self-tune re-applies softly and pauses after a click; load-more kicks harder until a batch of visible posts appears.

items.name: Simpler Aggression slider label
items.description: The slider label reads plainly strict / balanced / aggressive instead of "balanced (~28% hidden)", which overflowed and clipped the popup row.

items.name: Mute / Solo per-filter mixer
items.description: Each filter has independent Mute (hide this kind) and Solo (show only this kind) toggles; Solo wins over Mute.

items.name: Learning AI-slop scorer
items.description: Scores structural "tells" of AI-written prose and learns from Show-anyway / confirm / scroll-past signals; adjustable sensitivity; export / import / reset of the on-device model.

items.name: Author memory (mute + allowlist)
items.description: Mute an author (always hide) or allowlist one (always show), with a Profile quick-link and top hidden sources on the options page.

items.name: Custom filters
items.description: User-defined rules — words / phrases, regexes, hashtags, and companies / authors — applied on top of the built-in filters.

items.name: "Load more posts" helper
items.description: A grafted button that drives LinkedIn's own infinite-scroll loader so a batch that is entirely filtered doesn't dead-end the feed.

items.name: Detection-transparency panel
items.description: A read-only options-page view over the scorer: each structural tell with a plain-English description and your current learned weight, plus the full curated phrase banlist, searchable.

items.name: First-run welcome page
items.description: Opens once on install with instructions to pin FeedHacker to the toolbar (Chrome won't let an extension pin itself); pinning stays optional.

items.name: Collapsible options panels
items.description: Options-page sections (Activity, Insights, detection panel, Custom filters, Advanced) are collapsible and default to collapsed.

items.name: Remove remote banlist
items.description: Dropped the "use the latest banlist from GitHub" toggle, its setting, and the raw.githubusercontent.com host permission; the extension uses only its bundled banlist.

items.name: Company / brand posts filter
items.description: Mute (or Solo) posts authored by a LinkedIn Company or School page — corporate content that isn't a paid "Promoted" ad and isn't AI slop. Off by default.

items.name: "Always show" (whitelist) button
items.description: A one-click allowlist button on each stub — the author's posts are never filtered again — plus a removable Whitelist section on the options page.

items.name: Stub icon-button controls
items.description: A compact icon cluster on each stub: green splat (confirm AI slop + train), grey eye-strike (hide post), red mic-slash (mute author), blue eye (always show), box-arrow (visit profile), each with a tooltip.

items.name: Muted authors soft-blocked
items.description: A muted author's posts are hidden outright with no "Muted author" stub, so they simply stop appearing. Unmute from the options page.

items.name: Heartbeat focus-aware fix
items.description: The DOM-break heartbeat only counts empty scans while the feed tab is visible and focused, so a backgrounded or unfocused tab no longer trips the "selectors out of date" alarm.

items.name: Remove per-filter Aggressive toggle
items.description: Dropped the per-filter "A" toggle; the AI-slop sensitivity slider is now the single control for how aggressively slop is filtered, with the old aggressive rules folded in as weaker evidence.

items.name: Move the Enable switch into the header
items.description: Moved the master Enable toggle to the top-right of the popup header — a prominent kill switch that no longer pushes the rest of the controls down.

items.name: "Show sample" independent of "Show author" (+ rename)
items.description: Renamed the stub option "+ sample" → "Show sample" and made it stand alone — it no longer depends on Show author; every Feed-display checkbox is independent.

items.name: Group the AI-slop controls into a section
items.description: Gave AI slop its own section (blue header) holding the sensitivity slider and Hide AI-slop comments, instead of scattering those controls down the popup.

items.name: Fold the M / S key into the Posts header
items.description: Dropped the standalone "M mute · S solo" legend line and folded the key into the Posts section header, so the toggles are labelled in place and the top of the popup is calmer.

items.name: Rename "Names" → "Show author"
items.description: Renamed the stub display toggle from "Names" / "Name names" to "Show author (who + category)" so its job is obvious — it reveals who posted the suppressed item.

items.name: Rename "Details & activity" → "Advanced Settings"
items.description: Renamed the popup footer link that opens the Chrome options page from "Details & activity" to "Advanced Settings", which says what it is.

items.name: One-click Windows self-update
items.description: In Advanced Settings, an Update now button drives the whole update from inside the extension via a per-user native-messaging helper — no Chrome restart. Windows sideload build only; store build unchanged.

items.name: Toggle-driven stub + splat confirms & hides
items.description: The stub shows just the rule by default; Show author / Show sample add lines. The green AI-slop splat now confirms, trains, and hides the row (swaps to a checkmark).

items.name: Remove digest / "Collapse hidden content"
items.description: The digest mode (grouping runs of same-category hidden posts) was removed — its "Show these" button didn't reliably un-collapse. Later superseded by FH-007.

items.name: Sticky Hide / Show-anyway
items.description: Hidden and revealed posts no longer pop back when settings re-apply — re-applies preserve your explicit per-post choices instead of doing a full reset.

items.name: Context-invalidation teardown
items.description: After a Chrome update or reload, the orphaned content script shuts down cleanly — disconnects the observer, clears timers and listeners, removes injected UI, reveals hidden posts — instead of churning and logging errors for the life of the tab.

items.name: Bundled banlist / smaller page footprint
items.description: The banlist ships compiled into a bundled content script instead of a web-accessible file, so web_accessible_resources is empty — no extension-origin resource a site can enumerate — and boot is slightly faster.

items.name: Update now — hot store update, no restart
items.description: Extends the one-click self-update to Chrome Web Store installs: 'Check for updates' shows an 'Update now' button that applies the published store version in place via Chrome's update API (requestUpdateCheck → runtime.reload) — no reinstall, and in the following release no Chrome restart. If a newer version is on GitHub but not yet approved on the store, it says so instead of failing.
